2605.00422.title: BWLA: Breaking the Barrier of W1AX Post-Training Quantization for LLMs
2605.00422.paper_summary: Large language models (LLMs) have driven major progress in NLP, yet their substantial memory and compute demands still hinder practical deployment. Binarization can compress weights to 1 bit, fundamentally lowering compute and bandwidth cost. However, existing methods cannot address activation heavy tails and thus must keep activations in high precision, preventing true end-to-end acceleration. To overcome this limitation, we propose BWLA (Binarized Weights and Low-bit Activations), the first post-training quantization framework that preserves high accuracy while achieving 1-bit weight quantization together with low-bit activations (e.g., 6 bits). The Orthogonal-Kronecker Transformation (OKT) learns an orthogonal mapping via EM minimization, converting unimodal weights into symmetric bimodal forms while suppressing activation tails and incoherence. The Proximal SVD Projection (PSP) then performs lightweight low-rank refinement through proximal SVD projection, further enhancing quantizability with minimal overhead. On Qwen3-32B, BWLA reaches a Wikitext2 perplexity of 11.92 under 6-bit activations (vs. 38 from SOTA), improves five zero-shot tasks by more than 70%, and delivers 3.26 times inference speedup, demonstrating strong potential for real-world LLM compression and acceleration.
2605.00422.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）在自然語言處理（NLP）方面推動了重大進展，但其龐大的記憶體和計算需求仍然阻礙了實際部署。二元化可以將權重壓縮到1位元，從根本上降低計算和帶寬成本。然而，現有的方法無法解決激活重尾問題，因此必須保持高精度的激活，這妨礙了真正的端到端加速。為了克服這一限制，我們提出了BWLA（Binarized Weights and Low-bit Activations），這是第一個在後訓練量化框架中保持高準確度的同時實現1位元權重量化和低位元激活（例如，6位元）。正交克羅內克轉換（OKT）通過EM最小化學習正交映射，將單峰權重轉換為對稱雙峰形式，同時抑制激活尾部和不一致性。接下來，近端SVD投影（PSP）通過近端SVD投影進行輕量級的低秩精煉，進一步以最小的開銷增強可量化性。在Qwen3-32B上，BWLA在6位元激活下達到Wikitext2的困惑度為11.92（相比於SOTA的38），在五個零樣本任務中提高了70%以上，並實現了3.26倍的推理加速，顯示出在現實世界中進行LLM壓縮和加速的強大潛力。
2605.00422.author: Zhixiong Zhao et.al.
2605.00422.authors: Zhixiong Zhao, Zukang Xu, Dawei Yang
